campaign_brief.objective: Establish LSAD (Low-Signature Autonomous Detection) as a credible, program-ready capability among DoD program offices and defense primes, generating qualified engagement with acquisition and PEO stakeholders within two fiscal quarters.
campaign_brief.target_audience: DoD program managers, PEO/PM technical leads, DARPA/DIU innovation officers, and Tier-1 prime contractor systems engineers responsible for ISR and force protection portfolios.
campaign_brief.key_message: LSAD delivers passive, low-signature autonomous threat detection that closes the sensing gap in contested, GPS-denied environments without revealing the operator.
campaign_brief.proof_point: Demonstrated 92% detection accuracy across 30km of contested terrain in independent live-fire field trials with zero RF emission, validated against MIL-STD-810H environmental conditions.
campaign_brief.call_to_action: Request a classified-capable technical briefing and field demonstration at your facility or a designated test range.

primary_persona.role: DoD Program Manager / PEO Technical Lead for ISR & Force Protection
pain_points: Sensing and detection systems fail or degrade in GPS-denied and EW-contested environments
Active emitters expose force position and create counter-detection vulnerability
Acquisition pressure to field capability faster than the standard 5-7 year cycle allows
Difficulty validating TRL maturity of vendor claims before committing program dollars
information_sources: Defense News, Breaking Defense, and C4ISRNET
Industry days, AUSA, SOFIC, and Sea-Air-Space conferences
Peer program offices and FFRDC/JHU-APL technical advisors

buying_committee.their_question: Does this reduce schedule and integration risk for my program of record?
buying_committee.content_needed: TRL assessment summary, integration roadmap, and acquisition pathway brief (OTA/SBIR Phase III)

buying_committee.their_question: Can it integrate with our existing C2 and sensor fusion architecture?
buying_committee.content_needed: Interface control document overview, open-architecture (MOSA) compliance statement, API/data-format spec sheet

buying_committee.their_question: Is there a clean, fundable contract vehicle and is the vendor compliant?
buying_committee.content_needed: CMMC/ITAR compliance attestation, available contract vehicle list, pricing model and cost-realism backup

buying_committee.their_question: Will this work and survive in my real operating environment?
buying_committee.content_needed: Field trial video, soldier-touchpoint usability summary, MIL-STD ruggedization test results

icp.company_size: DoD program offices and primes with $50M+ ISR/force-protection portfolios; 500+ personnel for prime integrators
geographies: Five Eyes partner nations (UK, Australia, Canada)

messaging_hierarchy.headline: Detect Everything. Reveal Nothing.
messaging_hierarchy.subheadline: LSAD: passive autonomous detection for contested, GPS-denied operations.
messaging_hierarchy.body_message: When active sensors expose your force and EW shuts down your GPS, LSAD keeps you sensing. Its passive, low-signature autonomous architecture detects threats across contested terrain without emitting a single watt — proven in independent field trials and built MOSA-compliant for rapid integration into your program of record.
messaging_hierarchy.tone: Authoritative, technically precise, mission-focused, understated confidence

channels.channel: Defense industry conferences & demo days (AUSA, SOFIC, DIU events)
channels.rationale: Government acquisition relationships are built face-to-face; live demos at trusted venues drive credibility with PMs and primes.
channels.kpi: Number of qualified PM/PEO meetings booked

channels.channel: Targeted ABM to program offices & primes (LinkedIn + direct outreach)
channels.rationale: Buying committees are small and named; account-based targeting reaches specific PEOs and systems engineers efficiently.
channels.kpi: Engaged target accounts and committee-level meetings

channels.channel: Defense trade media & thought-leadership PR (Breaking Defense, C4ISRNET)
channels.rationale: PMs validate vendors through trusted defense press; earned and sponsored content builds capability awareness.
channels.kpi: Earned placements and referral traffic to gated assets

channels.rationale: Acquisition pathways (SBIR/OTA/DIU) are entry points; presence on solicitation channels surfaces fundable opportunities.
channels.kpi: Solicitation responses and RFI/RFP invitations received

channel_recommendation.channel_sequence: Begin with PR and thought-leadership to seed credibility 6-8 weeks pre-launch, then activate ABM outreach concurrently, time a flagship live demo to a major conference (launch event), and sustain with SBIR/portal presence and ABM nurture post-launch.

allocation.channel: Targeted ABM to program offices & primes

content_creative_plan.hero_asset: Field-trial capability film: a 4-minute documentary-style video of LSAD detecting threats across 30km of contested terrain in a GPS-denied range exercise, with on-screen telemetry and operator testimony (releasable, unclassified cut + classified-capable extended brief).

supporting_assets.purpose: Arm systems engineers with integration and MOSA-compliance detail
supporting_assets.specs: 8-12 pages, ITAR-cleared, includes ICD overview and TRL assessment
1 page, lists OTA/SBIR Phase III/DIU vehicles, compliance attestations
Transportable LSAD unit, ruggedized display, scripted scenario runbook

supporting_assets.purpose: Seed defense-press credibility on contested-environment sensing
supporting_assets.specs: 3 articles, 800-1200 words, bylined by chief engineer

supporting_assets.purpose: Persona-specific nurture for buying committee roles

content_calendar.action: Publish first thought-leadership article on EW-contested sensing gap
Launch ABM sequences to top 40 target program offices and primes
Release hero field-trial film (unclassified cut) to defense press under embargo
Flagship live demo at industry conference; PR push goes live
Distribute technical capability brief to engaged accounts; book demos
Publish second article + acquisition pathway one-pager syndication
Submit responses to active SBIR/DIU solicitations; nurture warm committees

actions: Complete ITAR clearance of all releasable assets and finalize unclassified hero film cut
Build named target account list of program offices and primes; map buying committees
Seed defense press relationships and place first thought-leadership article
Secure conference demo slot and prepare transportable demo kit
Execute flagship live demo at major defense conference
Release hero film and coordinated PR across Breaking Defense/C4ISRNET
Activate full ABM outreach to all target accounts
Stand up gated landing page with classified-briefing request CTA
Run technical briefings and on-site demos with engaged program offices
Submit to active SBIR/OTA/DIU solicitations aligned to FY funding windows
Sustain second/third article series and committee-level nurture
Report pipeline of qualified PM meetings and RFI/RFP invitations monthly

launch_timeline.defense_timing_note: US Government fiscal year runs Oct 1–Sep 30. Time outreach to influence the budget cycle: PMs shape requirements and explore new capability in Q1-Q2 (Oct-Mar), so seed LSAD then. Year-end 'use it or lose it' funds free up in Q4 (Jul-Sep) — position demos and OTA proposals by late May/June to capture expiring FY dollars. Avoid heavy launch activity during continuing-resolution (CR) periods (typically Oct-Dec) when new-start funding is frozen; emphasize SBIR/OTA vehicles that are CR-resilient.

sales_enablement.one_pager_summary: LSAD is a passive, low-signature autonomous detection system that maintains threat sensing in GPS-denied, EW-contested environments without emitting — eliminating the counter-detection risk of active sensors. Field-proven at 92% detection accuracy over 30km, MIL-STD-810H qualified, and MOSA-compliant for rapid integration into existing programs of record via OTA, SBIR Phase III, or DIU pathways.
talk_track: Open by anchoring on the contested-environment problem: 'When GPS goes down and active sensors paint a target on your force, what keeps you sensing?'
Introduce LSAD as the passive, zero-emission answer and state the field-trial proof point (92% / 30km / zero RF).
Connect to the PM's program: 'It's MOSA-compliant and TRL-mature, so it bolts into your existing C2 architecture without a multi-year integration.'
Lower acquisition friction: 'We're available on OTA and SBIR Phase III vehicles, and we're CMMC/ITAR compliant — so funding doesn't stall you.'
Close on a concrete next step: 'Let's get a classified-capable briefing and a demo on your range — when can we put it in your environment?'

objection_handling.objection: Your TRL claims are unverified — how do I know this isn't vaporware?
objection_handling.response: We can share independent third-party field-trial data validated against MIL-STD-810H, and we'll bring the live unit to your designated range so your team verifies performance firsthand before any funding commitment.

objection_handling.objection: Integrating a new sensor into our program of record is too risky and slow.
objection_handling.response: LSAD is built MOSA-compliant with a published ICD and standard data formats, so it integrates into your existing sensor-fusion and C2 stack without a proprietary lock-in or multi-year re-architecture — we'll walk your systems engineers through the interface in the technical brief.

objection_handling.objection: We don't have a contract vehicle or budget for a new start this fiscal year.
objection_handling.response: We're available on OTA, SBIR Phase III, and DIU pathways that are resilient to continuing resolutions and don't require a new-start authorization — and we can align proposal timing to capture FY-end funds. Let's identify the right vehicle for your office.

objection_handling.objection: How is this different from the active sensors we already field?
objection_handling.response: Active sensors emit, which exposes your force to counter-detection in contested theaters. LSAD is fully passive — zero RF emission — so you detect threats without revealing your position, which is exactly the survivability gap active systems can't close.

demo_sequence: Set the scenario: brief the operational use case (GPS-denied, contested terrain) the demo replicates
Show passive detection live: trigger threat events and display real-time detection with telemetry, emphasizing zero emission
Demonstrate autonomy and resilience: show performance under simulated EW/jamming conditions
Walk integration: display data output feeding a representative C2/fusion interface via the published ICD
Close with acquisition path: map the demonstrated capability to the customer's program and available contract vehicle, schedule on-range follow-up

kpis.primary_kpi: Number of qualified program-office/PEO engagements that convert to a technical briefing or RFI/RFP invitation

channel_kpis.kpi: Qualified PM/PEO meetings booked from demos

channel_kpis.channel: Targeted ABM to program offices & primes
channel_kpis.target: 20 of 40 target accounts engaged at 2+ committee roles

channel_kpis.kpi: Earned placements + referral traffic to gated assets

kpis.reporting_cadence: Weekly pipeline pulse to sales leadership; monthly executive dashboard against primary KPI and channel KPIs; quarterly review aligned to FY budget cycle.

risks.risk: Continuing resolution freezes new-start funding, stalling program-office engagement
risks.mitigation: Prioritize CR-resilient vehicles (OTA, SBIR Phase III, DIU) in messaging and proposals; shift outreach to requirements-shaping conversations during CR rather than funding asks.

risks.risk: ITAR/classification constraints limit how much capability detail can be shown publicly, slowing credibility-building
risks.mitigation: Maintain a cleared unclassified asset tier for public channels and reserve detailed performance data for classified-capable briefings under appropriate facility clearance/NDA.

risks.risk: Live demo underperforms in field conditions, damaging credibility with PMs
risks.mitigation: Run full dress rehearsals at the target range conditions, pre-stage redundant hardware, and script graceful fallback to validated recorded trial data if live conditions fail.

risks.risk: Long government sales cycles delay measurable pipeline beyond campaign window
risks.mitigation: Set leading-indicator KPIs (briefings, RFIs) rather than closed contracts; build a 12-month nurture track so momentum carries past the 6-month spend.

pivot_triggers: Fewer than 10 qualified PM meetings by end of week 6 — reallocate conference budget to direct ABM and targeted SBIR submissions
Continuing resolution extends past Q1 — pivot all messaging and asset emphasis to CR-resilient OTA/SBIR pathways
Earned PR placements fall below 3 by week 8 — shift PR budget to sponsored thought-leadership and syndicated whitepapers
Demo performance issues at flagship event — pause live-demo spend, pivot to recorded validated-trial briefings until hardware is re-qualified
